text.content:
Foundry allows systems to define a roll data for Actors and Items, allowing users to easily access useful data belonging to them, such as an attribute score or natural Defense.

In Shadow of the Weird Wizard, you can either use a shortcut to access a value (see further below) or a full path starting with @system, such as @system.stats.health.current.

So, for example, you can use 1d20 + @strMod or 1d20 + @system.attributes.str.value to achieve a Strength roll dice expression without boons or banes.

As of Foundry core v14, you can also use roll data keys as the value for an Active Effect Change.

Shortcuts for Common Keys

The keys below are hard coded shortcuts for commonly used keys, like attribute scores and modifiers, as well as stats, such as Defense or Level.

If you opt to not use any of the shortcuts below, you always use @system with their full path, as explained above.

Attributes

@str: system.attributes.str.value

@strMod: system.attributes.str.value - 10

@agi: system.attributes.agi.value

@agiMod: system.attributes.agi.value - 10

@int: system.attributes.int.value

@intMod: system.attributes.int.value - 10

@wil: system.attributes.wil.value

@wilMod: system.attributes.wil.value - 10

Defense

@def: system.stats.defense.total

@defNatural: system.stats.defense.natural

@defArmored: system.stats.defense.armored

Health

@health: system.stats.health.current

@healthNormal: system.stats.health.normal

@healthLost: system.stats.health.lost

Damage Total

@damage: system.stats.damage.value

@damageHalf: Math.floor(system.stats.damage.value / 2)

Speed

@speed: system.stats.speed.current

@speedNormal: system.stats.speed.normal

Other Useful Stats

@level: system.stats.level

@size: system.stats.size

@bonusDamage: system.stats.bonusdamage
